images.caption: CLAUDE.md configuration hierarchy in VS Code showing project-level and user-level files

images.caption: The three-tier instruction hierarchy showing when each mechanism loads and its context cost

images.caption: Multi-MCP chain: agent reads from Figma, processes tokens, and writes to Paper in one session
images.alt_text: Flow diagram showing an AI agent chaining two MCP servers: reading design specifications from Figma MCP, processing and normalizing design tokens, then writing the transformed output to Paper MCP canvas in a single agent session

images.caption: Orchestrator agent decomposing a dashboard design into spatial sub-tasks for parallel workers
images.alt_text: Terminal output showing an orchestrator agent decomposing a dashboard design into three spatial sub-tasks: header region assigned to worker-1, sidebar to worker-2, and main content to worker-3 for parallel execution

images.caption: OpenPencil canvas showing merged output from three concurrent agent teams on one dashboard
images.alt_text: OpenPencil editor showing a completed dashboard design with three color-coded regions indicating where concurrent agent teams worked: header agent, sidebar agent, and main content agent results merged into a unified layout

images.caption: Agent design review workflow checking output against design system constraints and flagging issues
images.alt_text: Terminal output showing an agent performing automated design review, checking generated components against design token specifications, and flagging two violations: incorrect border radius and non-standard color usage with suggested corrections

images.caption: Decompose-Delegate-Merge orchestration pattern with conflict resolution in the merge step
images.alt_text: Orchestration flow diagram showing the Decompose-Delegate-Merge pattern: a design brief enters the orchestrator which splits into three parallel worker agents, their outputs merge with conflict resolution for spacing and style consistency, producing the final unified design

images.caption: Agent generating a React component from Paper MCP get_jsx output with Tailwind classes
images.alt_text: Terminal session showing an AI agent calling the Paper MCP get_jsx tool for a hero section, receiving JSX output with Tailwind CSS classes, and creating a React component file with the generated code

images.caption: Agent accessibility audit showing WCAG 2.1 AA pass/fail results with automated fix suggestions
images.alt_text: Terminal output from an automated accessibility audit showing 18 passed WCAG 2.1 AA criteria and 3 failed criteria including missing alt text on images and insufficient color contrast, each with specific element selectors and suggested fixes

images.caption: The five-stage deploy pipeline from design artifact to production with agent and human roles
images.alt_text: Pipeline flow diagram showing five stages from design artifact to production: Paper canvas design, JSX and Tailwind export, responsive and accessible adaptation, human visual review, and Vercel deployment with agent responsibilities marked at stages two through four

images.alt_text: Side-by-side comparison of a hero section as designed in Paper canvas on the left and as rendered on the deployed production website on the right, demonstrating high visual fidelity between design and live output

images.caption: Case Study 1 architecture: Paper + Claude Code + MCP pipeline from Figma tokens to deployed site

images.caption: Before and after: traditional design workflow (5 days) vs agent-driven workflow (4 hours)
images.alt_text: Side-by-side workflow comparison showing a traditional seven-step manual design process spanning five days on the left and a three-step agent-driven process using Paper and Claude Code completing in four hours on the right

images.caption: Timeline comparison of four case studies showing design, build, iterate, and ship phases
images.alt_text: Gantt-style timeline showing four case study workflows as parallel tracks with design, build, iterate, and ship phases: marketing site in 4 hours, pitch deck in 2 hours, launch video in 3 hours, and component library over 8 hours across three days

images.caption: Ecosystem evolution from GUI-only design (2024) through agentic emergence (2026) to autonomous systems (2028)

images.caption: Design task spectrum from human-driven to agent-driven with 2026 current state and 2028 projections
images.alt_text: Horizontal spectrum diagram showing design tasks plotted from fully human-driven on the left to fully agent-driven on the right, with current 2026 positions marked and projected 2028 positions showing tasks like component generation and accessibility moving further right toward automation
